identificationModule.briefTitle: Effects and Safety of Diabetic GUideline Algorithm Implementation Performed by Primary Care Physicians in the Community
identificationModule.officialTitle: Effects and Safety of GUideline Algorithm Based Intervention on CaRdiovascular and Renal Outcomes in Elderly Diabetic Patients With High Cardiovascular Risk in the Community- A Cluster Randomized Controlled Trial (GUARD-Community Study)

descriptionModule.briefSummary: The Effects and Safety of Diabetic GUideline Algorithm Implementation in the Community (GUARD-Community) study is a 2-arm, cluster-randomized control trial to evaluate the effect and safety of guideline algorithm intervention performed by primary care physicians on cardiovascular and renal outcomes in elderly patients with high risk in community.
descriptionModule.detailedDescription:
Diabetes is an important public health concern. Elderly diabetic patients are characterized by a long duration and complications, including chronic kidney disease and/or cardiovascular disease. In the past 30 years, the guidelines of CDS, EASD or ADA have been frequently updated. The latest guideline on pharmacological algorithm recommend that patients with cardiovascular, renal disease or very high/high CV risk patients should be treated with anti-diabetic drugs presenting target organ protection, including SGLT2i and GLP1RA. And the guideline recommend comprehensive control of the cardiovascular risk factors, such as hypertension and dyslipidemia.

This GUARD-Community study is a community based cluster-randomized controlled trial and will enroll 5600 or more participants in more than 120 clusters aged ≥ 65 years with T2DM and complicated with high/very high cardiovascular risk factors . The trial will evaluate the the effects and safety of intensive "Guideline" algorithm implementation on CVD and renal outcomes. The primary hypothesis is that guideline algorithm intervention implemented by primary care physicians will significantly reduce the risk of 4-point MACE (comprised of cardiovascular death, nonfatal myocardial infarction, nonfatal stroke or hospitalization of heart failure) rates. In Phase 1 study, the control of blood sugar, blood pressure and lipids will be evaluated at 18 months after intervention. In Phase 2 study, the CVD and renal outcomes will be evaluated at 3 years. The study will last for 4 years.

designInfo.interventionModelDescription: Guideline based comprehensive management on the diabetic patients. In brief, SGLT2i or GLP-1RA will be used in priority in elderly diabetic patients with very high/high CV risk, and blood pressure controlled under 130/80mmHg， LDL-c\<1.8mmol/L in the very high-risk patients or \<2.6mmol/L in the high risk patients according to ESC/EASD guidelines.

maskingInfo.maskingDescription: Outcome Assessment Committee members will be blinded to outcome assignment.

armGroups.label: Intensive guideline algorithm implementation
armGroups.description: SGLT2i or GLP-1RA recommended in priority in subjects at very high/high CV risk. The targets of intervention will be achieved at HbA1C \<7%, blood pressure \<130/80mmHg， LDL-c\<1.8mmol/L at very high CV risk or \<2.6mmol/L at high CV risk patients, antiplatelet as secondary prevention of ASCVD.

armGroups.label: Conventional guideline algorithm implementation
armGroups.description: Treatment based on the current approaches implemented by local physicians(primary care physicians). Guideline based education and consults will be conducted to primary care physicians.

interventions.name: Intensive guideline algorithm implementation
interventions.description: Diabetes guideline pharmacological algorithm will be implemented by primary care physicians in community. In brief, SGLT2i or GLP-1RA will be recommended to control blood glucose in priority when subjects at very high/high CV risk and meet the target HbA1C\<7%, control blood pressure \<130/80mmHg， LDL-c\<1.8mmol/L at very high CV risk patients or \<2.6mmol/L at high CV risk patients, and antiplatelet as secondary prevention of ASCVD.

interventions.name: Conventional guideline algorithm implementation
interventions.description: The guideline intervention is based the guidance which the local physicians followed through self learning and education. The management of diabetes paitients will be decided by local physicians.

primaryOutcomes.measure: Primary Outcome of Phase 1: Comprehensive management effect of various cardiovascular risk factors in T2D，meeting control targets for a combination of A1c, BP, LDL-C.
primaryOutcomes.description: The proportion of participants with HbA1C\<7.0%, blood pressure\< 130/80 mm Hg，LDL-c\<1.8mmol/L at very high CV risk or \<2.6mmol/L at high CV risk.

primaryOutcomes.measure: Primary Outcome of Phase 2: Composite of 3P MACE and hospitalization for heart failure.
primaryOutcomes.description: Time to occurrence of cardiovascular and cerebrovascular death, non-fatal myocardial infarction, non-fatal Stroke, hospitalization for heart failure.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Secondary Outcome of Phase 1: Glycemic control rate
secondaryOutcomes.description: The proportion of participants with tight glucose control, targeting HbA1c \<7.0%

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Secondary Outcome of Phase 1: Mean HbA1C changes
secondaryOutcomes.description: Mean HbA1C changes of participants

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Secondary Outcome of Phase 1: Mean systolic and diastolic pressure changes
secondaryOutcomes.description: Mean systolic and diastolic pressure changes of participants

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Secondary Outcome of Phase 1: Mean LDL-c changes
secondaryOutcomes.description: Mean LDL-c changes of participants

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Secondary Outcome of Phase 1: Adherence to guideline algorithm medication recommendation rate
secondaryOutcomes.description: Use electronic medical recorded prescription and questionnaires to assess the proportion of participants who adhere to guideline recommended medication

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Secondary Outcome of Phase 2: Incident or worsening nephropathy
secondaryOutcomes.description: Time to composite of incident macroalbuminuria (UACR \>300 mg/g), a sustained decline in eGFR (decrease in the eGFR of 30% or more to a value of less than 60 when baseline ≥60ml per minute per 1.73 m2, decrease in the eGFR of 50% or more when baseline \<60ml per minute per 1.73 m2)from baseline, or chronic renal replacement therapy, or renal death.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Secondary Outcome of Phase 2: Cardiorenal composite endpoint
secondaryOutcomes.description: Time to eGFR (CKD-EPI formula) decrease, renal replacement therapy, renal or cardiovascular death

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Secondary Outcome of Phase 2: 3P MACE
secondaryOutcomes.description: Time to events occurence: cardiovascular death, non-fatal myocardial infarction, non-fatal stroke

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Secondary Outcome of Phase 2: New onset of macroalbuminuria.
secondaryOutcomes.description: Time to UACR\>300mg/g

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Secondary Outcome of Phase 2: Changes of myocardial ischemia in electrocardiogram (ECG)
secondaryOutcomes.description: Participants number of ECG ischemia demonstration occurence: ST-T segment depression more than 0.1mv in two adjacent leads of ECG compared with baseline, poor R wave progression.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Secondary Outcome of Phase 2: New onset of albuminuria
secondaryOutcomes.description: Time to UACR increase from \<30mg/g to ≥30mg/g

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Secondary Outcome of Phase 2: Albuminuria progression
secondaryOutcomes.description: Time to albuminuria progression: UACR increased by ≥30% and grade progression (ie, from normal to micro or macro, or from micro to macro)

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Secondary Outcome of Phase 2: Albuminuria regression
secondaryOutcomes.description: Time to albuminuria regression: UACR grade regression(ie, from macro to micro or normal, or from micro to normal), and the UACR value decreases by more than or equal to 30%

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Secondary Outcome of Phase 2: Changes in the ratio of patients with normal or abnormal urine protein at the end of the study
secondaryOutcomes.description: Rate change of normal or abnormal UACR. Normal means UACR\<30mg/g. Abnormal means UACR≥30mg/g

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Secondary Outcome of Phase 2: Slope of eGFR decline
secondaryOutcomes.description: Decrease of the eGFR over time

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Secondary Outcome of Phase 2: Retinopathy changes
secondaryOutcomes.description: Occurrence or regression of retinopathy(ETDRS-DRSS)

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Secondary Outcome of Phase 2: Body weight change
secondaryOutcomes.description: Absolute weight change and the percentage change of body weight

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Secondary Outcome of Phase 2: Changes of fatty liver prevalence
secondaryOutcomes.description: Rate change of fatty liver.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Secondary Outcome of Phase 2: Changes in beta-cell function
secondaryOutcomes.description: Absolute change assessed by HOMA2-%β method

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Secondary Outcome of Phase 2: Changes in cognitive function
secondaryOutcomes.description: Improvement or progression of cognitive function: The Mini-CogTM scale

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Secondary Outcome of Phase 2: The FRAIL scale
secondaryOutcomes.description: Changes of the simple frailty questionnaire score

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Secondary Outcome of Phase 2: All-cause death
secondaryOutcomes.description: Time to the death due to any cause

otherOutcomes.description: Cost-effectiveness analysis： quantification of Incremental Cost Ratio Life Cycle (ICER) and Quality Adjusted Years (QALYs)
Biomarkers associated with diagnosis or prognosis: using "omics" screening.
Gene polymorphism testing for drug response or prognosis: using genome-wide association study(GWAS) screening.

otherOutcomes.measure: The time rate of glycemic target range

locations.facility: Health Center of Xiangcheng Tourism Resort
